reasoning: The ICE BofA US Corporate Index option-adjusted spread currently sits at 0.84 as of November 7, 2025, having risen from 0.77 a week earlier. This near-term spike strongly hints at short-term mean reversion downward, meaning there is an elevated probability that spreads will retrace slightly by the Nov 16 resolution date (with statistical models pointing to a ~35% chance of being above 0.84). As time extends out to one and three months (Dec 9 and Feb 7), the risk structure becomes more balanced. Because 0.84 is historically very low (in the lowest percentile historically), a random-walk process with a bounded floor implies that the probability of spreads rising relative to the current level slowly drifts back up towards 0.5 as horizon extends, giving the probabilities 0.37, 0.43, and 0.48, accurately reflecting increasing uncertainty.
